req.title: Lab order routing (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's preferred lab when available. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
error_pathways: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers lab order routing via POST /ServiceRequest with preferred-lab resolution, fallback logic, permission checks, mandatory field validation, and state-transition guards. I selected: (1) EP positive – valid clinician order routes to preferred lab; (2) NEG – non-clinician role is denied; (3) NEG – missing mandatory element rejected; (4) DT – preferred lab unavailable triggers fallback; (5) STC – duplicate order in terminal state is rejected; (6) BVA – order payload at exact field-length boundary is accepted.

tests.name: test_order_routes_to_preferred_lab_when_available
preconditions: Patient P001 has preferred lab LAB-A registered in the system
LAB-A is marked active and available
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with valid payload: subject=P001, code=LOINC:2160-0, requester=Clinician-1, intent=order
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-HLT-068: order routed to preferred lab when available
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_order_routes_to_preferred_lab_when_available(clinician_auth_fixture, base_url, preferred_lab_setup):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture(role="clinician")
    preferred_lab_setup(patient_id="P001", lab_id="LAB-A", available=True)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "draft",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P001"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2160-0"}]},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/Clinician-1"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "ServiceRequest"
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    performers = [p["reference"] for p in body.get("performer", [])]
    assert any("LAB-A" in p for p in performers), f"Expected LAB-A in performers, got {performers}"

tests.name: test_non_clinician_role_denied_order_creation
preconditions: Authenticated token for a user with role=patient available via <patient_auth_fixture>
Patient P002 exists in the system
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with otherwise valid payload using patient-role token
Capture HTTP response status and OperationOutcome body
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_non_clinician_role_denied_order_creation(patient_auth_fixture, base_url):
    token = patient_auth_fixture(role="patient")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "draft",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P002"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2160-0"}]},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/Clinician-1"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    codes = [issue.get("code") for issue in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert any(c in ("forbidden", "security") for c in codes), f"Unexpected issue codes: {codes}"

tests.name: test_order_rejected_when_mandatory_code_element_missing
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with payload omitting the mandatory 'code' element
Capture HTTP response status and OperationOutcome
expected: issue[0].details or diagnostics references missing 'code' field
trace: R-HLT-068: missing mandatory elements negative path
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_order_rejected_when_mandatory_code_element_missing(clinician_auth_fixture, base_url):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture(role="clinician")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "draft",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P003"}
        # 'code' intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues)
    diagnostics = " ".join(str(i.get("diagnostics", "") or i.get("details", "")) for i in issues)
    assert "code" in diagnostics.lower(), f"Expected 'code' in diagnostics, got: {diagnostics}"

tests.name: test_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable
preconditions: Patient P004 has preferred lab LAB-B registered but LAB-B is marked inactive/unavailable
System default lab LAB-DEFAULT is active
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with valid payload for Patient P004
Inspect performer reference in response body
expected: Extension or note indicates fallback was applied
trace: R-HLT-068: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable
R-HLT-068: error pathways / decision table condition
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable(
    clinician_auth_fixture, base_url, preferred_lab_setup
):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture(role="clinician")
    preferred_lab_setup(patient_id="P004", lab_id="LAB-B", available=False)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "draft",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P004"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2160-0"}]},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/Clinician-1"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    performers = [p["reference"] for p in body.get("performer", [])]
    assert any("LAB-DEFAULT" in p for p in performers), f"Expected LAB-DEFAULT, got {performers}"
    assert not any("LAB-B" in p for p in performers), "LAB-B should not be assigned when unavailable"

tests.name: test_duplicate_order_on_completed_request_rejected
preconditions: ServiceRequest SR-DONE exists with status=completed for Patient P005
actions: PUT /ServiceRequest/SR-DONE with same payload attempting to re-activate order (status=draft)
expected: Diagnostics indicate invalid state transition from 'completed'
trace: R-HLT-068: conflicting state transitions negative path
R-HLT-068: STC from completed back to draft is invalid
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_duplicate_order_on_completed_request_rejected(
    clinician_auth_fixture, base_url, completed_order_fixture
):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture(role="clinician")
    order_id = completed_order_fixture(patient_id="P005", status="completed")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "id": order_id,
        "status": "draft",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P005"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2160-0"}]},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/Clinician-1"}
    }
    resp = requests.put(f"{base_url}/ServiceRequest/{order_id}", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (409, 422), f"Expected 409 or 422, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    diagnostics = " ".join(str(i.get("diagnostics", "")) for i in body.get("issue", []))
    assert any(kw in diagnostics.lower() for kw in ("state", "transition", "completed"))

tests.name: test_order_with_note_text_at_max_boundary_length_accepted
preconditions: Patient P006 has preferred lab LAB-A available
System maximum note text length is defined as 1000 characters (per API contract)
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with a note.text of exactly 1000 characters
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: note[0].text length equals 1000 characters in persisted resource
trace: R-HLT-068: boundary at max-length mandatory/optional element
R-HLT-068: BVA on note field upper boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

MAX_NOTE_LEN = 1000

def test_order_with_note_text_at_max_boundary_length_accepted(
    clinician_auth_fixture, base_url, preferred_lab_setup
):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture(role="clinician")
    preferred_lab_setup(patient_id="P006", lab_id="LAB-A", available=True)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    boundary_note = "A" * MAX_NOTE_LEN
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "draft",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P006"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2160-0"}]},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/Clinician-1"},
        "note": [{"text": boundary_note}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "ServiceRequest"
    saved_note = body.get("note", [{}])[0].get("text", "")
    assert len(saved_note) == MAX_NOTE_LEN, f"Expected note len {MAX_NOTE_LEN}, got {len(saved_note)}"
